claim_text: Transcription factor EB induces transcription of pro-inflammatory cytokines in macrophages infected with Staphylococcus aureus.

runs.response_text:
VERDICT: SUPPORT

The first abstract directly states that "TFEB was also rapidly activated in murine macrophages upon S. aureus infection and was required for proper transcriptional induction of several proinflammatory cytokines and chemokines." This explicitly demonstrates that TFEB (Transcription Factor EB) induces transcription of pro-inflammatory cytokines in macrophages infected with Staphylococcus aureus, exactly matching the claim.

VERDICT: NEI  
The abstracts discuss the involvement of transcription factors (TFEB and others) in host defense and macrophage responses to bacterial infections, but none provide direct evidence about TFEB specifically inducing pro-inflammatory cytokines in macrophages infected with Staphylococcus aureus.

VERDICT: SUPPORT
Abstract [doc_id 24177706] states that TFEB was required for the transcriptional induction of several proinflammatory cytokines and chemokines in murine macrophages upon S. aureus infection.

VERDICT: SUPPORT
